name: Sierra de Magallanes y Ermita de Santa Bárbara — circular desde Higuera de la Sierra
region_intro: Jogging routes around Higuera De La Sierra traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and dense woodlands, offering varied terrain for runners. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often leading through forested areas and past historical sites. These paths provide opportunities to experience the natural environment and local scenery. The area's topography makes for challenging running, with several routes featuring notable ascents. Best jogging routes around Higuera De La Sierra The most popular jogging route is Sierra de Magallanes y Ermita de Santa Bárbara — circular desde Higuera de la Sierra, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Magallanes. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador de Castañuelo – Mirador del Castañar loop from Aracena, a difficult 16.1 miles (25.9 km) path. This trail offers extensive running through varied terrain, including viewpoints over the landscape. Local runners also love the Aracena town centre – Historic Center of Aracena loop from Aracena, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail leading through the historic center and surrounding areas, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Jogging around Higuera De La Sierra is defined by hilly terrain, forested paths, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance running. The routes in Higuera De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 130 runners have used komoot to explore Higuera De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Higuera De La Sierra?
A: The running routes in Higuera De La Sierra are characterized by rolling hills and dense woodlands. You'll encounter varied terrain with significant elevation changes, often leading through forested areas and past historical sites. The topography makes for challenging running, with several routes featuring notable ascents.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance running trails in the area?
A: Yes, Higuera De La Sierra offers challenging long-distance options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castañuelo – Mirador del Castañar loop from Aracena&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 25.9 km path with extensive running through varied terrain and significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any moderate running loops suitable for a good workout?
A: Absolutely. You can find moderate running loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31453981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Corteconcepción&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 11.1 km with a moderate elevation gain, offering a good balance of challenge and enjoyment.

Q: What are some points of interest or landmarks I can see along the jogging routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013661773/sierra-de-magallanes-y-ermita-de-santa-barbara-circular-desde-higuera-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Magallanes y Ermita de Santa Bárbara — circular desde Higuera de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; route passes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Bárbara&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotto of the Marvels&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aracena Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there any running routes that go through historic areas?
A: Yes, you can explore historic areas while running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aracena town centre – Historic Center of Aracena loop from Aracena&lt;/a&gt; is a 14.9 km trail that leads directly through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Aracena&lt;/a&gt; and its surrounding areas.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Higuera De La Sierra?
A: The running routes in Higuera De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents, and the opportunity to experience the natural environment and local scenery.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013661773/sierra-de-magallanes-y-ermita-de-santa-barbara-circular-desde-higuera-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Magallanes y Ermita de Santa Bárbara — circular desde Higuera de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 11.6 km loop.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Higuera De La Sierra area?
A: Komoot offers a selection of five curated running routes around Higuera De La Sierra, ranging from moderate to difficult, providing options for different ability levels.

Q: Can I find a shorter, moderate running route near Aracena?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter, moderate option near Aracena, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Aracena – Aracena town centre loop from Aracena&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 7.1 km long and classified as moderate.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on running trails in Higuera De La Sierra?
A: The region is known for its hilly terrain, so you can expect significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castañuelo – Mirador del Castañar loop from Aracena&lt;/a&gt; involves an ascent of over 790 meters, while a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31453981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Corteconcepción&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of around 200 meters.

Q: Are there any running routes with scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region's topography provides several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castañuelo – Mirador del Castañar loop from Aracena&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for its extensive running through varied terrain, including viewpoints over the landscape.
tips: The Hermitage of Santa Bárbara is located at the top of the Sierra de Santa Bárbara, in Higuera de la Sierra. Although the hermitage is unfinished, it has become one of the best viewing points for contemplating the town and its surroundings.
Corriol surrounded by dry stone walls, idyllic for running and enjoying the area near La Umbría.

Higuera de la Sierra is an excellent option to enjoy nature and culture in the province of Huelva. This charming municipality is located in the Sierra de Huelva region and is part of the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park.
Don't miss the Church of San Sebastián, an 18th-century building listed as a Site of Cultural Interest. You can also visit the Hermitage of San Antonio and the Chapel of Cristo del Rosario.
